qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: User-controlled flow fs_path enables arbitrary server file overwrite
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets flow create/update filesystem writes in `api/v1/flows.py`, not the advisory's missing authentication on the three `api/v1/monitor.py` endpoints.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated profile picture path traversal exposes local files
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a path traversal read in `api/v1/files.py`; although it is unauthenticated, it does not involve the advisory's `monitor.py` endpoints or their missing `get_current_active_user` dependency.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Multipart filename traversal in v2 upload writes outside user storage root
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns authenticated file-upload path traversal in `api/v2/files.py` and `services/storage/local.py`, not missing authentication on `api/v1/monitor.py`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's root cause: missing `Depends(get_current_active_user)` authentication on `GET /api/v1/monitor/messages`, `GET /api/v1/monitor/transactions`, and `DELETE /api/v1/monitor/messages/session/{session_id}` in `src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/monitor.py`.
evidence: The advisory JSON explicitly describes three vulnerable handlers in `src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/monitor.py` that are missing the `get_current_active_user` dependency.
`flow_fs_path_arbitrary_overwrite` targets `POST /api/v1/flows/` and `PATCH /api/v1/flows/{flow_id}` with attacker-controlled `fs_path` writes in `api/v1/flows.py`.

`profile_picture_path_traversal_lfi` targets `GET /api/v1/files/profile_pictures/{folder_name}/{file_name}` in `api/v1/files.py` and relies on path traversal rather than the advisory's missing auth on monitor endpoints.
`v2_file_upload_path_traversal_write` targets `POST /api/v2/files/` and the local storage backend write path, not any `api/v1/monitor.py` handler.
